methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
General Matter operates in the nuclear energy sector at Series A stage, having raised over $50M to date. The comparable universe consists of 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private nuclear energy companies including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

Stage-progression methodology is applicable and forms the primary valuation anchor. Using total raised ($50M) as a proxy for implicit valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression to subsequent funding rounds yields an implied valuation of $100M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient narrow comparables—while 8 same-sector private peers exist, applying same-stage or similar-tier filters yields fewer than the three datapoints required for meaningful median derivation. A broader all-sector approach would introduce undue noise given the specialized nature of nuclear technology.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $85M to $115M, with a median of $100M. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries material uncertainty around appropriate step-up multiples for the nuclear subsector, which may differ from generalized venture progression rates given capital intensity and regulatory complexity. The sparse comparable set in narrowly-defined tiers underscores valuation sensitivity to deal structure and market conditions at time of Series A close.
